title: Districts clip at 3x3 — buildings overflow off the map on 4th row
description:
## Problem

When a district (folder) contains enough files to require a 4th row of buildings, the district boundary does not expand to accommodate them. Buildings in the 4th+ row render outside the district bounds and hang off the edge of the map.

Observed in the live UI — districts work fine up to a 3x3 grid of buildings but break beyond that.

## Expected behavior

District boundaries should grow to fit all contained buildings regardless of count. The layout engine or renderer should produce a valid bounding box for any number of children.

## Where to look

- `internal/layout/` — treemap/packer may be clamping district dimensions
- `web/src/canvas/BuildingRenderer.ts` — renderer may clip to a fixed grid
- `web/src/store/cityStore.ts` — state may cap district geometry

## Repro

Point at a repo with a directory containing 10+ source files. Observe that buildings in the 4th row overflow the district boundary.

title: Occluded building portions render at reduced opacity for depth readability
description:
## Problem

In the isometric city view, shorter buildings partially hidden behind taller buildings in front are fully opaque. This makes it hard to perceive depth or understand the spatial layout — you can't tell what's behind a tall building without panning.

## Desired behavior

**Always-on occlusion fade:**
- When a portion of a building is geometrically obscured by a taller building in front of it (in isometric projection), that obscured region renders at ~0.7 opacity
- Only the actually-hidden pixels/region fades — the visible (non-occluded) portion of the same building stays at full 1.0 opacity
- This is a static rendering effect, always active during normal viewing

**Selection override:**
- When an occluded building is selected, it returns to full 1.0 opacity (the selection "punches through" the depth fade)
- This ensures the user can always see a building clearly once they've explicitly focused on it

**Not in scope:**
- Labels/text are not affected by this occlusion — only building geometry
- No camera panning or transparency on the front (occluding) building
- Front buildings remain fully opaque; only the back building's hidden region fades

## Implementation notes

The occlusion test is in screen space after isometric projection: for each building, determine which pixels/regions fall behind a taller building's projected silhouette. This is a per-frame calculation tied to camera position.

Possible approaches:
1. **Clip-path / masking** — render occluded buildings with a clip mask derived from the front building's silhouette, apply reduced opacity to the clipped region
2. **Depth buffer approach** — use painter's algorithm ordering (already in place for isometric) to detect overlap regions
3. **Simplified heuristic** — if a building's bounding box partially intersects a taller building's projected footprint in front of it, fade the intersecting vertical slice

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Portions of buildings hidden behind taller foreground buildings render at ~0.7 opacity
- [ ] Only the geometrically obscured region fades; visible portions stay at 1.0
- [ ] Selected buildings always render at full opacity regardless of occlusion
- [ ] Effect is always-on (no interaction trigger needed)
- [ ] Labels/text on buildings are unaffected
- [ ] No visible rendering artifacts at the boundary between faded and full-opacity regions
- [ ] Performance: no significant frame drop with the current building count

## Files likely involved

- `web/src/canvas/CityRenderer.ts` — building draw order, main render loop
- `web/src/canvas/BuildingRenderer.ts` — individual building rendering
- `web/src/canvas/camera.ts` — projection math for occlusion calculation
- `web/src/store/uiStore.ts` — selected building state (for the override)

title: Large buildings fully occlude smaller ones behind — need opacity fade for depth
description:
## Problem

When a tall building is drawn in front of a shorter one, the back building is completely hidden. In isometric view, buildings are sorted back-to-front by (gx + gy) and drawn in order (`BuildingRenderer.ts:71-77`), so front buildings paint over back ones with no transparency. There's no visual indication that a building exists behind a large one.

## Expected behavior

Buildings that are partially or fully occluded by a building in front of them should render with reduced opacity (or some other depth cue) so the user can still see them. The back building should be visible "through" or "around" the front building rather than completely disappearing.

## Approach options

1. **Depth-based opacity fade**: buildings further from the camera render at slightly lower opacity so overlapping areas show through. Simple but affects all buildings, not just occluded ones.

2. **Occluded building detection**: after sorting, detect which buildings' screen-space silhouettes are substantially covered by later (frontward) buildings, and lower opacity only on those. More targeted but more expensive.

3. **X-ray on hover/cursor**: when the cursor or selection is on an occluded building, temporarily fade the occluding building in front. This is the least invasive — normal rendering stays solid, occlusion is only resolved on interaction.

4. **Outline ghost**: draw occluded buildings as faint wireframe outlines visible through the front building. Matches the existing hidden-back-edge dashed line style (`BuildingRenderer.ts:127-143`).

## Files involved

- `web/src/canvas/BuildingRenderer.ts` — draw order and per-building rendering
- `web/src/canvas/CityRenderer.ts` — render loop, could add an occlusion pass
- `web/src/canvas/HitTester.ts` — may need updates if occluded buildings become clickable targets

title: HitTester staging slot mismatch with renderer
description:
HitTester.ts hitTestAgents() uses `!agent.fromId && !agent.targetId` (line 191) to decide
whether an agent is a staging agent for slot counter purposes, but AgentRenderer.drawAgents()
uses an else-fallthrough that catches any agent not matching the flying or hovering branches.

An agent with fromId set but no toId (or fromId+toId but flyProgress=undefined) would:
- In AgentRenderer: fall to drawStagingAgent, incrementing stagingSlotIndex
- In HitTester: NOT increment stagingSlot (isStagingAgent is false because fromId is truthy)
- In agentScreenPos: still fall to the staging branch and compute a position using slot=0

This causes two problems:
1. The partial-flight agent's hit-test position uses wrong slot (always 0)
2. All subsequent staging agents' hit-test positions are offset by one vs drawn positions

The fix: isStagingAgent should mirror the renderer's three-branch logic, not use a
simplified condition. The staging check should be the negation of both the flying and
hovering conditions combined.

Introduced in 3cd9468 (make staging and flying agents clickable).

title: GetState/SetState race loses concurrent updates
description:
In internal/agents/monitor.go lines 76-78, applySnapshot does:

  curr := cityState.GetState()
  curr.Agents = tracker.Agents(curr.Buildings)
  cityState.SetState(curr)

Meanwhile cmd/agentic-city/main.go lines 502-505 and 509-511 do:

  curr := state.GetState()
  // modify buildings
  state.SetState(next)

These run in separate goroutines. If the agent monitor's applySnapshot fires between main.go's GetState and SetState (or vice versa), the later SetState overwrites the earlier one's changes — e.g., building status updates are lost, or new agent discoveries are reverted.

The debounce (300ms) reduces frequency but does not prevent the race. Under filesystem churn + agent activity, updates will be silently dropped.

Expected: use a compare-and-swap pattern, a dedicated mutex around the read-modify-write, or consolidate state mutations into a single serial goroutine.

Introduced: 3952f89 (May 14). Not fixed by 73915e6 (debounce).

title: Test pass/fail counts always zero in live mode — same root cause as coverage
description:
## Problem

`RepoStats.TestsPassing` and `RepoStats.TestsTotal` are always zero in live mode. Same root cause as agentic-city-ksq (coverage showing zero).

## Root cause

`MetricsWatcher` is never started in live mode, so `MetricsSource.DirStatus` and `MetricsSource.FileStatus` are never populated. Additionally, `computeStats` in `internal/city/builder.go:137-160` only computes `FileCount`, `TotalLOC`, and `Coverage` — it never counts test pass/fail results even if metrics data were available.

Two gaps:
1. **No metrics data** — `MetricsWatcher` not wired up (covered by agentic-city-ksq)
2. **No test stat computation** — `computeStats` doesn't populate `TestsPassing` or `TestsTotal` from `MetricsSource.DirStatus`/`FileStatus`

## Fix

When agentic-city-ksq is implemented (wiring up MetricsWatcher), also:
- Count buildings with `status == "ok"` → `TestsPassing`
- Count buildings with `status != "unknown"` → `TestsTotal`
- Or derive directly from `MetricsSource.DirStatus` counts

## Files involved

- `internal/city/builder.go` — `computeStats` needs test count logic
- `internal/repo/metrics.go` — already parses test results, no changes needed
- Depends on agentic-city-ksq being completed first

title: RFC 6902 add on array overwrites instead of insert
description:
The frontend JSON Patch applicator in wsMiddleware.ts setAt() (line 56) treats RFC 6902
"add" at an array index as overwrite (arr[idx] = value) instead of insert (arr.splice).

RFC 6902 section 4.1 specifies: "If the target location specifies an array index, a new
value is inserted into the array at the specified index."

The backend (hub/state.go diffKeyedArray) correctly generates "add" ops with specific
indices when agents appear:
  { "op": "add", "path": "/agents/0", "value": {...} }

The frontend setAt() at line 56:
  arr[idx] = setAt(arr[idx], tail, value);

This overwrites the element at index 0 instead of inserting before it (Array.splice).
Result: when an agent joins, the frontend agent roster becomes corrupted. The agent at
the target index is silently replaced, and the array never grows to the correct length.

The same bug affects the "remove" path in removeAt() (line 73), though removeAt does use
splice correctly, so only "add" is broken.

Repro: start the server in demo mode, connect a browser, then add a new agent session
to a monitored repo. The full state snapshot works correctly, but the first "add" patch
will overwrite an existing agent.

Fix: change line 56 to use Array.splice for insert when tail.length === 0:
  if (tail.length === 0) { arr.splice(idx, 0, value); }
  else { arr[idx] = setAt(arr[idx], tail, value); }

title: Wire up MetricsWatcher so live mode shows real coverage and test status
description:
## Problem

In live mode, every building gets `Coverage: -1` (unknown) at scan time (`internal/repo/scanner.go:379`), and the `MetricsWatcher` is never started. The coverage feature shows zero for all files even when coverage data exists in the repo.

Demo mode works fine because `makeDemoBuildings` generates random coverage values directly.

## Root cause

`MetricsWatcher` (`internal/repo/metrics.go`) is fully implemented — parsers for Go coverage.out, LCOV, coverage.py JSON, JUnit XML, and Go test JSON all exist and are tested — but nothing in `main.go` creates or starts a `MetricsWatcher`. There is no path from the parsed metrics data to the building state.

Specifically:
- `BuildConfig` has no `MetricsConfig` field
- `main.go` has no CLI flags for coverage/test-result file paths
- No goroutine consumes `MetricsWatcher.Updates` to apply coverage/status to buildings

## Fix

1. Auto-detect common coverage/test-result files in the repo root (e.g. `coverage.out`, `lcov.info`, `coverage.json`, `test-results.xml`, `test-results.json`)
2. Add CLI flags (`--coverage`, `--test-results`) for explicit path overrides
3. Start a `MetricsWatcher` in `main.go` alongside the existing `runWatcher`
4. When metrics updates arrive, apply `MetricsSource.Coverage` and `StatusFor()` to each building in the city state and notify the hub

## Files involved

- `cmd/agentic-city/main.go` — wire up MetricsWatcher, add CLI flags
- `internal/city/builder.go` — add helper to apply MetricsSource to buildings
- `internal/repo/metrics.go` — already complete, no changes expected

title: Agents invisible in live mode — Tracker not wired up, sessionToAgent sets no TargetID
description:
## Problem

No UFOs appear on the live city map even when active Claude/Codex/Gemini sessions exist and are detected by agentwatch.

## Root cause

Two independent issues conspire:

1. **`monitor.go:sessionToAgent` sets no location fields.** It creates agents with ID, color, mode, task, progress, and modelTier — but `TargetID` and `LocationConfidence` are always empty. The comment on line 159-160 says: "specific file targeting is left for future work."

2. **`AgentRenderer.ts:89-93` skips agents without position data:**
   \`\`\`ts
   } else if (agent.targetId) {
     drawHoveringAgent(...);
   }
   // Agents with no position data are not drawn
   \`\`\`

So agentwatch discovers sessions correctly, `monitor.go` builds agent structs, they arrive in `CityState.Agents` — but the renderer drops them on the floor because they have no `targetId`.

## The fix already exists but isn't wired up

`tracker.go` has a complete `Tracker` type that:
- Correlates file-system events to agent working directories (`ObserveFileEvent`)
- Infers district-level location from `WorkingDir` (`districtFromWorkingDir`)
- Fills `TargetID` and `LocationConfidence` with priority: file-event > district > unknown
- Returns fully located `model.Agent` structs via `Tracker.Agents(buildings)`

But `Tracker` is never instantiated. `main.go` calls `StartMonitor` which uses the simpler `sessionToAgent` path instead of `Tracker.toAgent`.

## Fix

1. In `StartMonitor` (or `main.go`), create a `Tracker` and use `Tracker.Agents(buildings)` instead of `sessionsToAgents`
2. Feed file-system events from the existing repo watcher into `Tracker.ObserveFileEvent`
3. In the EventSink callback, call `tracker.UpdateSession` instead of the standalone `sessionsToAgents`
4. Ensure agents with `LocationConfidence: "unknown"` are still rendered (e.g. parked at a default location or shown in a staging area) so they're never invisible

## Files involved

- `internal/agents/monitor.go` — replace `sessionsToAgents` with `Tracker` usage
- `internal/agents/tracker.go` — already complete
- `cmd/agentic-city/main.go` — may need to pass file events from `runWatcher` to `Tracker`
- `web/src/canvas/AgentRenderer.ts` — consider rendering "unknown" agents at a fallback position

title: LOD zoom system — buildings collapse into district blocks at far zoom
description:
## Overview

When zoomed out far enough, individual file-buildings should collapse into larger aggregate buildings representing the district (directory). This matches the L3 MODULE level from the design spec in `code-sim/project/sketches/sketch-C-zoom-levels.jsx`.

The sketch defines 4 LOD levels:
- **L1 FUNCTION** — zoomed into a single function's code blocks
- **L2 FILE** — current default, individual buildings per file
- **L3 MODULE** — districts become single combined buildings (file count, aggregate LOC, worst-case status)
- **L4 CODEBASE** — orbital view, modules as satellite nodes around a core

This epic covers L2↔L3 transitions. L1 and L4 are separate future work.

## Acceptance criteria

- Zooming out past a threshold collapses file-buildings into a single district-building per directory
- District-building shows: directory name, file count, total LOC, aggregate coverage, worst-case status
- Agents on collapsed files relocate to the district-building surface
- Zooming back in smoothly expands back to individual buildings
- Keyboard zoom (`+`/`-`/`0`) triggers the same transitions

title: Update HUD and selection for L3 district-buildings
description:
HUD panels and keyboard selection need to work with district-buildings at L3.

- Arrow-key navigation cycles through district-buildings instead of file-buildings
- Selecting a district-building shows aggregate stats in the right rail (file count, total LOC, coverage, status breakdown)
- Pressing Enter on a selected district-building zooms in to L2 centered on that district
- Bottom strip should show current LOD level indicator

Files: `web/src/hud/RightRail.tsx`, `web/src/hud/BottomStrip.tsx`, `web/src/store/uiStore.ts`

title: Define LOD thresholds and add zoom-level state to uiStore
description:
Add a `lodLevel` field to `uiStore` that tracks the current LOD (L1–L4). Define zoom thresholds that trigger LOD transitions.

- Add `lodLevel: 'L1' | 'L2' | 'L3' | 'L4'` to the UI store
- Compute LOD from camera zoom value with hysteresis (avoid flickering at boundaries)
- Expose a derived selector `useLodLevel()` for renderers and HUD
- Keyboard shortcuts `+`/`-`/`0` and scroll/pinch zoom all feed the same zoom → LOD pipeline

Files: `web/src/store/uiStore.ts`, `web/src/hooks/useCameraControls.ts`

title: Compute aggregate district-buildings from city state at L3
description:
When LOD is L3, derive a set of district-buildings from the existing city state. Each district-building aggregates its child file-buildings.

- Total LOC = sum of children
- Coverage = weighted average of children with known coverage
- Status = worst-case (err > warn > unknown > ok)
- File count = number of children
- Geometry = district bounding box (already in District.GX/GY/GW/GH)
- Agent count = number of agents targeting files in this district

This should be a pure derived computation (selector or memo), not a mutation of the source data. The original file-buildings stay intact for when the user zooms back in.

Files: `web/src/store/cityStore.ts` (new selector), possibly `web/src/store/lodStore.ts` (new)

title: Render district-buildings in BuildingRenderer at L3 zoom
description:
When `lodLevel === 'L3'`, the canvas should render district-buildings instead of file-buildings.

Per the sketch-C spec:
- Each district renders as a single larger building with hatched window pattern (vertical lines representing combined files)
- District label in bold with file count badge
- Status color on border (cyan=ok, amber=warn, red=err, red flash for err)
- Agent dots clustered on the district-building surface
- Status indicator dot (blinking for warn/err)

The renderer should switch cleanly between L2 (file-buildings) and L3 (district-buildings) based on the LOD selector, not by re-laying-out — just by choosing which building set to draw.

Files: `web/src/canvas/BuildingRenderer.ts`
Spec: `code-sim/project/sketches/sketch-C-zoom-levels.jsx` (L3 MODULE quad)

title: Animate L2↔L3 LOD transitions
description:
Smooth visual transition when crossing the L2↔L3 LOD boundary.

- Zooming out: file-buildings fade/shrink while the district-building fades/grows in
- Zooming in: reverse — district-building dissolves into individual file-buildings
- Agents should smoothly reposition from file locations to district surface (and back)
- Transition duration should match the animation timings in `code-sim/project/sketches/sd-helpers.jsx`
- No layout recalculation during transition — purely visual interpolation

Files: `web/src/canvas/BuildingRenderer.ts`, `web/src/store/uiStore.ts`
Spec: `code-sim/project/sketches/sd-helpers.jsx` (animation timings)

title: Occlusion fade overlay missing sort-key guard
description:
In web/src/canvas/BuildingRenderer.ts, drawOcclusionFadeOverlay() (commit 82dc974) iterates sorted[j] for j > i without checking that front.gx+front.gy is strictly greater than back.gx+back.gy. The comment on line 723 says 'strictly higher sort key' but no code enforces it.

Compare with findPassiveOccluders() in OcclusionDetector.ts line 63:
  if (front.gx + front.gy <= backKey) continue;

The drawOcclusionFadeOverlay inline loop at lines 725-731 has no equivalent guard. Buildings with equal sort keys at j > i in the sorted array will be incorrectly treated as occluders, producing spurious ghost overlays for buildings that are at the same depth.

Expected: add 'if ((front.gx + front.gy) <= (back.gx + back.gy)) continue;' before the overlap check, matching findPassiveOccluders behavior.

Build SHA: 8d17f55

title: FlyProgress omitempty causes frame glitch
description:
In demo mode, when a flying agent completes its arc (FlyProgress >= 1.0), the demo ticker
at cmd/agentic-city/main.go:429 sets FlyProgress = 0.0 and swaps FromID/ToID.

Because model.Agent.FlyProgress uses `json:"flyProgress,omitempty"` and Go's omitempty
treats float64(0.0) as empty, the 0.0 value is omitted from the JSON sent to the frontend.

On the frontend, the agent has fromId and toId set but flyProgress is undefined. The
renderer condition `agent.flyProgress !== undefined` (AgentRenderer.ts line 131) evaluates
to false, causing the flying agent to fall through to drawStagingAgent for one tick (100ms).
On the next tick, FlyProgress advances to ~0.014 and serializes normally, so the agent
resumes flying.

Result: every time a flying agent completes a loop in demo mode, it flashes to the staging
area for one frame before reappearing on the flight path. The same issue could affect live
agents if the backend ever sets FlyProgress exactly to 0.0.

Fix: either remove omitempty from FlyProgress in model.Agent, or use a sentinel like -1
for "not flying", or set the initial FlyProgress to a small positive epsilon.

Build: f27250d
Files:
- /home/mrf/Projects/agentic-city/internal/model/model.go line 84 (omitempty on FlyProgress)
- /home/mrf/Projects/agentic-city/cmd/agentic-city/main.go line 429 (sets FlyProgress=0.0)

title: Clicking a different building does not update the selected panel
description:
## Problem

Clicking on a different file/building does not update the selected building box (right rail panel and/or cursor highlight on the map).

## Click → selection pipeline

The full chain is:

1. **Click handler** (`App.tsx:72-103`): calls \`hitTestBuildings(renderer.camera, city.buildings, sx, sy)\`
2. **Hit test** (`HitTester.ts:61-73`): sorts buildings back-to-front, returns last hit (frontmost)
3. **State update** (`App.tsx:95-96`): calls \`setCursor(hit.id)\` and \`selectBuilding(hit.id)\`
4. **Store** (`uiStore.ts:126`): \`selectBuilding: (id) => set({ selectedBuildingId: id })\`
5. **Sync to renderer** (`App.tsx:192-196`): useEffect syncs \`selectedBuildingId\` to renderer
6. **Visual cursor** (`CityRenderer.ts:136-143`): draws cursor highlight for \`cursorBuildingId\`
7. **Right rail** (`RightRail.tsx:249-250`): reads \`selectedId ?? cursorId\`, finds building by ID

## Areas to investigate

### Drag suppression (most likely)
`App.tsx:75`: \`if (dragRef.current?.hasDragged) return\` — the drag threshold is only 4 cumulative pixels (\`DRAG_THRESHOLD = 4\`). This is cumulative distance, not net displacement — small hand tremor across multiple mousemove events adds up and could suppress the click. Even returning to the start position counts. This would silently bail out of the click handler without updating selection.

### HUD overlay interception
The right rail (\`position: fixed, zIndex: 90, width: 220px\`) covers the right edge of the canvas. Buildings behind the rail are visible but clicks hit the rail div, not the canvas. Similarly TopBar, BottomStrip, and LeftRail could intercept clicks near edges.

### Hit test geometry mismatch
The hit test uses the building's 6-vertex silhouette (\`HitTester.ts:33-45\`). If the camera has been panned/zoomed but the buildings array is stale (briefly out of sync between WebSocket update and React re-render), the silhouette might not match what's drawn.

## Suggested fix for drag threshold

Change from cumulative distance to net displacement:
\`\`\`ts
// Current (fragile):
drag.totalDist += Math.hypot(e.movementX, e.movementY);
if (!drag.hasDragged && drag.totalDist < DRAG_THRESHOLD) return;

// Better (net displacement from mousedown point):
const netDist = Math.hypot(e.clientX - drag.startX, e.clientY - drag.startY);
if (!drag.hasDragged && netDist < DRAG_THRESHOLD) return;
\`\`\`

## Files involved

- \`web/src/App.tsx\` — click handler, drag detection
- \`web/src/canvas/HitTester.ts\` — hit test geometry
- \`web/src/store/uiStore.ts\` — selection state
- \`web/src/hud/RightRail.tsx\` — selected panel display
- \`web/src/canvas/CityRenderer.ts\` — cursor highlight rendering

title: UFO ↔ agent list cross-linking: click UFO highlights list entry, click list highlights UFO
description:
## Problem

UFOs on the canvas and their descriptions in the agent list (HUD sidebar) are visually disconnected. There's no way to associate a UFO with its agent details or vice versa. When many agents are active, it's hard to tell which UFO belongs to which list entry.

## Desired behavior

**Canvas → List (click UFO):**
- Clicking a UFO on the canvas highlights/selects the corresponding agent entry in the HUD agent list
- The list scrolls to bring the entry into view if needed

**List → Canvas (click description):**
- Clicking an agent entry in the HUD list visually highlights the corresponding UFO on the canvas (glow, pulse, outline, or color shift)
- No camera pan — the UFO highlights in place wherever it currently is

**Keyboard equivalents (required — keyboard-first contract):**
- Arrow keys navigating the agent list simultaneously highlight the corresponding UFO on the canvas
- A canvas-side keybinding (e.g., Tab or bracket keys) cycles through UFOs, updating the list selection to follow
- Focus indicators must be visible on both the list entry and the UFO

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Clicking a UFO selects + scrolls-to its agent list entry
- [ ] Clicking an agent list entry highlights the corresponding UFO (no camera pan)
- [ ] Keyboard navigation in the list highlights corresponding UFOs in real-time
- [ ] A canvas keybinding cycles UFO selection, list follows
- [ ] Both highlight states have visible focus indicators
- [ ] Highlight clears when clicking empty space or pressing Escape
- [ ] New bindings documented in BottomStrip.tsx and `?` overlay

## Files likely involved

- `web/src/App.tsx` — click handlers, selection state bridging
- `web/src/store/uiStore.ts` — selected agent state
- `web/src/canvas/AgentRenderer.ts` — UFO highlight rendering
- `web/src/hud/LeftRail.tsx` or agent list component — list selection + scroll-into-view
- `web/src/canvas/HitTester.ts` — UFO hit testing (if not already implemented)
- `web/src/hud/BottomStrip.tsx` — keybinding documentation
